content.full_text:
THE COMMITTEE TO ELECT
RICH WISEMAN
MENDOCINO COUNTY SHERIFF-CORONER
PO BOX 884 UKIAH, CA. 95482
TELEPHONE (707) 462-8018

March 15, 1990

Fair Political
Practices Commission
Attn: John Rothman
428 J. Street Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear John,

On March 14, 1990 at 9:30 a.m. I contacted the FPPC Technical
Assistance Unit and was subsequently transferred to your Legal
Assistance Unit and ultimately to you.

I advised you that I was Rich Wiseman and I was a candidate
for Mendocino County Sheriff. I told you that I had placed a
photograph of myself in uniform in my brochure and that the
question had been raised that this may not be legal. You advised
me that this question had come up in the past and that you did not
know of any state law or FPPC violation. That it wasn't a
violation of the Political Reform Act and that unless the county
had a specific ordinance against placing a photograph in uniform
in a brochure that I should be safe.

I haven't found any specific provision stating you can't wear
your uniform in a brochure, therefore, I assume I have acted in
good faith.

I should also note that prior to placing my photograph in the
brochure I reviewed past practices by other Sheriff's candidates
in this county and adjoining counties who have placed their
photograph in uniform in their brochures. Due to this past
practice I felt confident to use a photograph of me in uniform in
my brochure.

I thank you for your attention to this matter. I would
appreciate your confirmation of this conversation in writing as
soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Rich Wiseman
Candidate for Sheriff
County of Mendocino
